view_decl:
pub struct PageAllocatorView {
    pub page_array: Seq<<Page as View>::V>,
    pub free_pages_4k: Set<PagePtr>,
    pub free_pages_2m: Set<PagePtr>,
    pub free_pages_1g: Set<PagePtr>,
    pub allocated_pages_4k: Set<PagePtr>,
    pub allocated_pages_2m: Set<PagePtr>,
    pub allocated_pages_1g: Set<PagePtr>,
    pub mapped_pages_4k: Set<PagePtr>,
    pub mapped_pages_2m: Set<PagePtr>,
    pub mapped_pages_1g: Set<PagePtr>,
    pub container_map_4k: Map<ContainerPtr, Set<PagePtr>>,
    pub container_map_2m: Map<ContainerPtr, Set<PagePtr>>,
    pub container_map_1g: Map<ContainerPtr, Set<PagePtr>>,
}

impl View for PageAllocator {
    type V = PageAllocatorView;
    closed spec fn view(&self) -> PageAllocatorView {
        PageAllocatorView {
            page_array: self.page_array@.map(|i: int, p: Page| p@),
            free_pages_4k: self.free_pages_4k@.to_set(),
            free_pages_2m: self.free_pages_2m@.to_set(),
            free_pages_1g: self.free_pages_1g@.to_set(),
            allocated_pages_4k: self.allocated_pages_4k@,
            allocated_pages_2m: self.allocated_pages_2m@,
            allocated_pages_1g: self.allocated_pages_1g@,
            mapped_pages_4k: self.mapped_pages_4k@,
            mapped_pages_2m: self.mapped_pages_2m@,
            mapped_pages_1g: self.mapped_pages_1g@,
            container_map_4k: self.container_map_4k@,
            container_map_2m: self.container_map_2m@,
            container_map_1g: self.container_map_1g@,
        }
    }
}
issues: Uses @/<Page as View>::V for Array, Page, and StaticLinkedList even though those dependency views are listed as uncovered; this will fail typecheck unless those View impls actually exist.
page_array is declared as Seq<Page::V> and mapped with p@ despite Page having no known view, so the viewed_type depends on a missing dep view.

`self.seq@@` is not a valid/justified element-wise projection: after unwrapping `Ghost<Seq<A>>`, `Seq<A>` is already a spec sequence and `@` does not automatically map elements to `A::V`; the body likely has type `Seq<A>` or fails, not `Seq<A::V>`.

view_decl:
pub struct ProcessView {
    pub owning_container: ContainerPtr,
    pub rev_ptr: SLLIndex,
    pub pcid: Pcid,
    pub ioid: Option<IOid>,
    pub owned_threads: <StaticLinkedList<ThreadPtr, MAX_NUM_THREADS_PER_PROC> as View>::V,
    pub parent: Option<ProcPtr>,
    pub parent_rev_ptr: Option<SLLIndex>,
    pub children: <StaticLinkedList<ProcPtr, PROC_CHILD_LIST_LEN> as View>::V,
    pub uppertree_seq: Seq<ProcPtr>,
    pub subtree_set: Set<ProcPtr>,
    pub depth: usize,
    pub dmd_paging_mode: DemandPagingMode,
}

impl View for Process {
    type V = ProcessView;
    closed spec fn view(&self) -> ProcessView {
        ProcessView {
            owning_container: self.owning_container,
            rev_ptr: self.rev_ptr,
            pcid: self.pcid,
            ioid: self.ioid,
            owned_threads: self.owned_threads@,
            parent: self.parent,
            parent_rev_ptr: self.parent_rev_ptr,
            children: self.children@,
            uppertree_seq: self.uppertree_seq@,
            subtree_set: self.subtree_set@,
            depth: self.depth,
            dmd_paging_mode: self.dmd_paging_mode,
        }
    }
}
issues: StaticLinkedList is marked uncovered/no View in scope, but owned_threads and children require `<StaticLinkedList<...> as View>::V` and `self.field@`, which is a hard typecheck error.
cpu_list uses self.cpu_list@ and declares Seq<Cpu>, but Array is listed as uncovered with no known view, so this dependency view is missing/unsupported.

view_decl:
pub struct ThreadView {
    pub owning_container: ContainerPtr,
    pub owning_proc: ProcPtr,
    pub state: <ThreadState as View>::V,
    pub proc_rev_ptr: SLLIndex,
    pub scheduler_rev_ptr: Option<SLLIndex>,
    pub blocking_endpoint_ptr: Option<EndpointPtr>,
    pub blocking_endpoint_index: Option<EndpointIdx>,
    pub endpoint_rev_ptr: Option<SLLIndex>,
    pub running_cpu: Option<CpuId>,
    pub endpoint_descriptors: <Array<Option<EndpointPtr>, MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS> as View>::V,
    pub ipc_payload: <IPCPayLoad as View>::V,
    pub error_code: Option<<RetValueType as View>::V>,
    pub trap_frame: <TrapFrameOption as View>::V,
}

impl View for Thread {
    type V = ThreadView;
    closed spec fn view(&self) -> ThreadView {
        ThreadView {
            owning_container: self.owning_container,
            owning_proc: self.owning_proc,
            state: self.state@,
            proc_rev_ptr: self.proc_rev_ptr,
            scheduler_rev_ptr: self.scheduler_rev_ptr,
            blocking_endpoint_ptr: self.blocking_endpoint_ptr,
            blocking_endpoint_index: self.blocking_endpoint_index,
            endpoint_rev_ptr: self.endpoint_rev_ptr,
            running_cpu: self.running_cpu,
            endpoint_descriptors: self.endpoint_descriptors@,
            ipc_payload: self.ipc_payload@,
            error_code: self.error_code@,
            trap_frame: self.trap_frame@,
        }
    }
}
issues: Uses `@` and `<ThreadState as View>::V` for uncovered `ThreadState`; no dependency view is known.
Uses `@` and `<Array<...> as View>::V` for uncovered `Array`; no dependency view is known.
Uses `@` and `<IPCPayLoad as View>::V` for uncovered `IPCPayLoad`; no dependency view is known.
Uses `self.error_code@` / `<RetValueType as View>::V` for uncovered `RetValueType`; no dependency view is known.
Uses `@` and `<TrapFrameOption as View>::V` for uncovered `TrapFrameOption`; no dependency view is known.

view_decl:
pub struct MemoryManagerView {
    pub kernel_entries: Seq<usize>,
    pub kernel_entries_ghost: Seq<<PageEntry as View>::V>,
    pub free_pcids: Set<Pcid>,
    pub pcid_to_proc_ptr: Seq<Option<ProcPtr>>,
    pub page_tables: Seq<Option<<PageTable as View>::V>>,
    pub page_table_pages: Map<PagePtr, Pcid>,
    pub free_ioids: Set<IOid>,
    pub ioid_to_proc_ptr: Seq<Option<ProcPtr>>,
    pub iommu_tables: Seq<Option<<PageTable as View>::V>>,
    pub iommu_table_pages: Map<PagePtr, IOid>,
    pub root_table: <RootTable as View>::V,
    pub root_table_cache: Seq<Seq<Seq<Option<(IOid, usize)>>>>,
    pub pci_bitmap: <PCIBitMap as View>::V,
}

impl View for MemoryManager {
    type V = MemoryManagerView;
    closed spec fn view(&self) -> MemoryManagerView {
        MemoryManagerView {
            kernel_entries: self.kernel_entries@,
            kernel_entries_ghost: self.kernel_entries_ghost@.map(|i: int, e: PageEntry| e@),
            free_pcids: self.free_pcids@.to_set(),
            pcid_to_proc_ptr: self.pcid_to_proc_ptr@,
            page_tables: self.page_tables@.map(|i: int, opt: Option<PageTable>| match opt { Some(pt) => Some(pt@), None => None }),
            page_table_pages: self.page_table_pages@,
            free_ioids: self.free_ioids@.to_set(),
            ioid_to_proc_ptr: self.ioid_to_proc_ptr@,
            iommu_tables: self.iommu_tables@.map(|i: int, opt: Option<PageTable>| match opt { Some(pt) => Some(pt@), None => None }),
            iommu_table_pages: self.iommu_table_pages@,
            root_table: self.root_table@,
            root_table_cache: self.root_table_cache@,
            pci_bitmap: self.pci_bitmap@,
        }
    }
}
issues: Uses `@`/`<T as View>::V` for uncovered dependency types (`Array`, `ArrayVec`, `PageEntry`, `PageTable`, `RootTable`, `PCIBitMap`), so the declared view likely requires missing `View` impls and will fail typecheck.

Projects primitive-containing containers through `@` on uncovered containers, e.g. `self.kernel_entries@`, `self.pcid_to_proc_ptr@`, and `self.free_pcids@`, despite `Array`/`ArrayVec` having no known view.

view_decl:
pub enum RetValueTypeView {
    SuccessUsize { value: usize },
    SuccessSeqUsize { value: Seq<usize> },
    SuccessPairUsize { value1: usize, value2: usize },
    SuccessThreeUsize { value1: usize, value2: usize, value3: usize },
    ErrorNoQuota,
    ErrorVaInUse,
    CpuIdle,
    Error,
    Else,
    NoQuota,
    VaInUse,
}

impl View for RetValueType {
    type V = RetValueTypeView;
    closed spec fn view(&self) -> RetValueTypeView {
        match *self {
            RetValueType::SuccessUsize { value } => RetValueTypeView::SuccessUsize { value },
            RetValueType::SuccessSeqUsize { value } => RetValueTypeView::SuccessSeqUsize { value: value@@ },
            RetValueType::SuccessPairUsize { value1, value2 } => RetValueTypeView::SuccessPairUsize { value1, value2 },
            RetValueType::SuccessThreeUsize { value1, value2, value3 } => RetValueTypeView::SuccessThreeUsize { value1, value2, value3 },
            RetValueType::ErrorNoQuota => RetValueTypeView::ErrorNoQuota,
            RetValueType::ErrorVaInUse => RetValueTypeView::ErrorVaInUse,
            RetValueType::CpuIdle => RetValueTypeView::CpuIdle,
            RetValueType::Error => RetValueTypeView::Error,
            RetValueType::Else => RetValueTypeView::Else,
            RetValueType::NoQuota => RetValueTypeView::NoQuota,
            RetValueType::VaInUse => RetValueTypeView::VaInUse,
        }
    }
}
issues: SuccessSeqUsize uses value@@; Ghost<Seq<usize>> should project to Seq<usize> with value@, and the extra @ attempts to view an already-spec Seq.
Uses self.data@ even though Array has no known View in scope, so the candidate view depends on an unavailable dependency projection.
